OBJECTIONS TO SEPARATE STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT JEFBAR

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JEFBAR S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE GROUND OF UNCONSCIONABILITY IS DENIED. UNCONSCIONABILITY HAS PROCEDURAL AND SUBSTANTIVE ELEMENTS; "BOTH MUST BE APPEAR FOR A COURT TO INVALIDATE A CONTRACT OR ONE OF ITS INDIVIDUAL TERMS."(SERPA V. CALIFORNIA SURETY INVESTIGATIONS, INC. (2013) 215 CAL. APP. 4TH 695, 703.) DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE CONTRACT WAS PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE. DEFENDANT COULD HAVE OBTAINED PHONE SERVICE FROM OTHER PHONE PROVIDERS. CURRIE-MARTINEZ DEC. 12, 13, AND 26. MR. CHRISTIAN IS AN EXPERIENCED BUSINESS PERSON. (LOSCH DEC., EX. L AT 27:19-34:14) (CHRISTIAN DEP.). HIS DECISION TO STAY WITH TELEKENEX AS A SERVICE PROVIDER CANNOT BE UNCONSCIONABLE BASED ON THE ALLEGED CONDUCT OF THE THIRD PARTY (SI&A) FROM WHOM JETBAR PURCHASED ITS BUSINESS DEMANDING THAT JETBAR QUICKLY SET UP ITS OWN PHONE SERVICE. FURTHER, DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN SURPRISE; THE "EARLY TERMINATION CHARGE" IS IN A PARAGRAPH TITLED AS SUCH IN THE MASTER SERVICE AGREEMENT ("MSA"), IN BOLD AND UNDERLINED. (CHRISTIAN DECL. 13, EX. A., P.3, 18.) ALL FOUR PAGES OF THE MSA ARE INITIALED BY MR. CHRISTIAN, AND THE LAST PAGE IS SIGNED BY HIM. HE READ IT CAREFULLY BEFORE SIGNING. (CURRIE-MARTINEZ DEC., 23 AND EXHS. D, E, F, G, AND H.) HAVING FAILED TO SHOW PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY, THE MOTION IS DENIED. IN ANY EVENT, EVEN IF ANY PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY HAD BEEN ESTABLISHED, DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY. THE ALLEGEDLY UNCONSCIONABLE CLAUSE IS THE EARLY TERMINATION CLAUSE. DEFENDANT HAS NOT IDENTIFIED FACTS TO SHOW THAT IT WAS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE AT THE TIME THE CONTRACT WAS ENTERED INTO. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO ANY OF THE EVIDENCE CITED IN THIS ORDER ARE OVERRULED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge Cynthia M. Lee; Clerk KCox; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-19-2013 Continued To Sep-23-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-03-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant JEFBAR S motion for summary judgment on the ground of unconscionability is denied. Unconscionability has procedural and substantive elements; "both must be appear for a court to invalidate a contract or one of its individual terms."(Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc. (2013) 215 Cal. App. 4th 695, 703.) Defendant has not shown that the contract was procedurally unconscionable. Defendant could have obtained phone service from other phone providers. Currie-Martinez Dec. 12, 13, and 26. Mr. Christian is an experienced business person. (Losch Dec., Ex. L at 27:19-34:14) (Christian Dep.). His decision to stay with Telekenex as a service provider cannot be unconscionable based on the alleged conduct of the third party (SI&A) from whom Jetbar purchased its business demanding that Jetbar quickly set up its own phone service. Further, Defendant has not shown surprise; the "early termination charge" is in a paragraph titled as such in the Master Service Agreement ("MSA"), in bold and underlined. (Christian Decl. 13, Ex. A., p.3, 18.) All four pages of the MSA are initialed by Mr. Christian, and the last page is signed by him. He read it carefully before signing. (Currie-Martinez Dec., 23 and Exhs. D, E, F, G, and H.) Having failed to show procedural unconscionability, the motion is denied. In any event, even if any procedural unconscionability had been established, Defendant has not shown substantive unconscionability. The allegedly unconscionable clause is the early termination clause. Defendant has not identified facts to show that it was substantively unconscionable at the time the contract was entered into. Defendant's objections to any of the evidence cited in this order are overruled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order to comply with CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
